onsetsample.Description: the onset time of the event in samples, relative to the start of the EEG (bdf) file

eventnumber.Description: event number relative to the task (pre-test, categorisation, post-test)

stimnumber.Description: ID number of the stimulus that was shown

stimname.Description: name of the stimulus that was shown

istarget.Description: stimulus is not a target (0), is a traget (1), is padding (2), used in the pre- and post-test only

time_stimon.Description: time the stimulus is presented, resets for each part

time_stimoff.Description: time the stimulus is taken away, resets for each part

stimfilename.Description: name of the stimulus that was shown

spatial_freq.Description: spatial frequency of the stimulus that was shown

pattern.Description: pattern of the stimulus that was shown

contrast.Description: contrast of the stimulus that was shown

shape.Description: shape of the stimulus that was shown

is_fast_stream.Description: is this a fast stream (pre- or post-test)(1) or the main categorisation task (0)

category.Description: stimulus category, as chosen by the pair

ppt_1_response.Description: category response of ppt 1 in the pair

ppt_1_keypressed.Description: response button used by ppt 1 in the pair

ppt_1_rt.Description: response time of ppt 1 in the pair

ppt_1_correct.Description: did ppt 1 in the pair give the correct response?

ppt_2_response.Description: category response of ppt 2 in the pair

ppt_2_keypressed.Description: response button used by ppt 2 in the pair

ppt_2_rt.Description: response time of ppt 2 in the pair

ppt_2_correct.Description: did ppt 2 in the pair give the correct response?

button1.Description: button mapping shown to the participants for button 1

button2.Description: button mapping shown to the participants for button 2

button3.Description: button mapping shown to the participants for button 3

button4.Description: button mapping shown to the participants for button 4

task_part_number.Description: part of the experiment: pre-test (1), main categorisation task (2), or post-test (3)
